mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to discount equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and residential demand, forcing earnings downgrades and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a directional hit rate below 40% across 2000-2007 walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle confirms that accommodative policy successfully stimulates real economic activity, driving short-term equity momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles do not yield positive 5-day forward returns, or the mechanism's predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 280, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 280, or the threshold shows no predictive edge over a linear baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a directional accuracy below 45% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sudden supply-side contraction that disrupts manufacturing output and supply chains, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO drops > 0.5%, or the threshold fails to separate winning from losing periods with statistical significance.
